title: One driver dead in NH collision between pickup truck and school bus
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A 74-year-old man died in a head-on collision between his pickup truck and a school bus in Marlborough, NH.
The school bus was carrying 13 students; none were seriously injured, but some were taken to the hospital as a precaution.
The crash occurred on Route 12; the pickup truck crossed into oncoming traffic and hit the bus head-on, according to police.

title: Carl Yastrzemski’s grandson, Braves’ Mike Yastrzemski, walks off Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Mike Yastrzemski's walk-off hit in the 10th inning led the Atlanta Braves to a 3-2 win over the Boston Red Sox.
Boston's early solo homers by Drake Baldwin and Michael Harris gave the Braves an initial 2-0 lead.
Red Sox starting pitcher Connelly Early allowed two runs over five innings, with six strikeouts and no walks.

title: Report: Reunion between Patriots, Stefon Diggs viewed as unlikely despite mutual interest
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Stefon Diggs is interested in rejoining the Patriots, but an A.J. Brown trade complicates the possibility.
Diggs had a 1,000-yard receiving season last year, but might not accept a third receiver role.
Patriots are prioritizing A.J. Brown, with his $32 million AAV contract affecting their salary cap decisions.

title: Boston police seek missing boy, 15
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: 15-year-old Lafayette LaGrant-Gomes was last seen Tuesday near 965 Blue Hill Ave in Dorchester, Boston police report.
He is described as a Black male, 5'8", thin build, with brown eyes and black hair, frequently visits key Boston locations.
Boston police urge anyone with information to contact District B-3 detectives or submit anonymous tips via CrimeStoppers.

title: Police: Teen allegedly shot girlfriend’s mother in the head on Mother’s Day
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Tyler Broomes, 18, was arrested for allegedly shooting his girlfriend's mother and another man in Dorchester on Mother's Day.
The victims, both 42, suffered gunshot wounds; the woman's injuries were life-threatening while the man's were not.
Broomes faces multiple charges, including assault with intent to murder, and is held without bail pending a May 21 hearing.

title: How would a team made up entirely of former Red Sox look in 2026?
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Former Red Sox players currently thriving include Chris Sale, Eduardo Rodriguez, Kyle Harrison, Michael Wacha, and Nathan Eovaldi with a combined 2.74 ERA.
The 2026 lineup of former Red Sox includes Mookie Betts, Xander Bogaerts, Kyle Schwarber, and breakout star Dominic Smith batting over .350.
Critics argue Boston could be more competitive if key players like Betts and Bogaerts had remained, given their current performances.

title: Red Sox’ Roman Anthony no longer wearing wrist brace, played catch Friday
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Roman Anthony, 22, of the Red Sox, has missed nine games due to a right wrist sprain.
Anthony is no longer wearing a wrist brace after receiving a cortisone injection, hinting at recovery progress.
Anthony plans to resume swinging soon after recently playing catch, starting rehab towards full on-field activity.

title: Jeep drives into Norwood day care
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A Jeep accidentally crashed into the entrance of BrightPath daycare in Norwood on Friday morning with no injuries reported.
The driver mistakenly hit the accelerator while dropping off a passenger, causing the SUV to roll into the building.
The daycare was temporarily closed for structural assessment, with damage described as 'moderate' by fire officials.

title: Here’s what experts are predicting the Patriots’ 2026 record will be
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Experts predict the New England Patriots will finish the 2026 regular season with a 10-7 record.
The Patriots' 2026 schedule includes challenging games against Seattle, Denver, and Chicago after an easier 2025 slate.
Sports Illustrated offers a less optimistic view, predicting a 9-8 record due to off-field issues and a tough schedule.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Mars Attacks!' is available for free streaming on YouTube and Plex.
'Marty, Life is Short' featuring Martin Short is streaming on Netflix.
'Welcome to Wrexham' Season 5 is available on Hulu.
